verbGlossary.INITIATED BY: {target} launches {source} by defining project requirements, scope, and success criteria on behalf of the client.
verbGlossary.LED BY: {target} holds primary accountability for {source}'s deliverables, schedule, and quality outcomes.
verbGlossary.MODELED BY: {target} creates or maintains the 3D/BIM model that represents the design intent for {source}.
verbGlossary.QUANTIFIED BY: {target} produces the detailed cost takeoff and budget estimate that defines {source}'s financial baseline.
verbGlossary.FILED BY: {target} prepares and submits the permit applications, regulatory forms, or approval packages required by {source}.
verbGlossary.ORCHESTRATED BY: {source} sequences and integrates multiple workstreams, subcontractors, and deliverables within {target}.
verbGlossary.BASELINED BY: {source} establishes the approved schedule, cost, or scope baseline against which phase progress is measured.
verbGlossary.SOLICITED BY: {source} issues bid invitations, manages the bid process, and collects proposals from subcontractors and suppliers.
verbGlossary.AWARDED BY: {target} executes the subcontract or purchase order that commits the project to a trade partner for {source} scope.
verbGlossary.TRACKED BY: {target} monitors submittal status, approval cycles, and revision history to keep {source} on schedule.
verbGlossary.REVIEWED BY: {target} evaluates {source} deliverable for conformance with design intent, specifications, or contract requirements.
verbGlossary.EXPEDITED BY: {source} accelerates fabrication, delivery, or approval timelines to prevent {target} from falling behind schedule.
verbGlossary.CROSS-CHECKED BY: {source} independently verifies {target}'s cost estimates, quantities, or budget assumptions against historical data and market pricing.
verbGlossary.RECEIVED BY: {source} physically accepts and verifies delivered materials at the jobsite against packing slips and specifications.
verbGlossary.DIRECTED BY: {source} makes real-time field decisions on work sequence, crew deployment, and resource allocation during {target}.
verbGlossary.CONDUCTED BY: {source} leads the orientation session, training class, or safety briefing that {target} requires.
verbGlossary.MONITORED BY: {source} observes {target}'s field conditions for safety hazards, environmental compliance, or quality deviations.
verbGlossary.SURVEYED BY: {target} performs the field layout, grade checks, or elevation surveys required to execute {source}.
verbGlossary.GRADED BY: {target} operates equipment or directs earthwork crews to achieve the specified grades and elevations for {source}.
verbGlossary.LAID OUT BY: {target} establishes control points, utility routing, and spatial references that guide {source}'s field installation.
verbGlossary.INSPECTED BY: {source} examines {target}'s completed work against acceptance criteria before it is covered or concealed.
verbGlossary.PLACED BY: {source} oversees or performs the concrete placement, reinforcement positioning, or material installation during {target}.
verbGlossary.SEQUENCED BY: {source} determines the erection order, crane picks, and connection sequence that govern how {target} proceeds in the field.
verbGlossary.CERTIFIED BY: {target} verifies that {source}'s safety systems, equipment, and personnel meet regulatory certification requirements.
verbGlossary.VERIFIED BY: {source} confirms that {target}'s field conditions (elevations, dimensions, embedments) match the approved drawings.
verbGlossary.FORMED BY: {source} builds and strips the formwork that shapes the concrete elements constructed during {target}.
verbGlossary.INSTALLED BY: {source} physically mounts, connects, or assembles the components that comprise {target}'s deliverable.
verbGlossary.DETAILED BY: {target} produces the shop drawings, profiles, and layout dimensions that guide fabrication and installation for {source}.
verbGlossary.TESTED BY: {target} performs physical tests (mortar, compression, adhesion) to verify {source}'s materials meet specification.
verbGlossary.WITNESSED BY: {source} observes a critical test, pour, or installation as an independent third party to confirm compliance.
verbGlossary.SCHEDULED BY: {target} sets the start date, duration, and sequencing of {source}'s activities within the master schedule.
verbGlossary.CLASH-CHECKED BY: {source} runs clash detection on the coordinated BIM model to resolve conflicts before {target}'s field installation begins.
verbGlossary.HUNG BY: {target} installs the ductwork, piping, or conduit supports from overhead structure during {source}.
verbGlossary.ROUGHED IN BY: {target} installs the concealed piping, wiring, or ductwork within walls and ceilings before {source} proceeds to finishes.
verbGlossary.ROUTED BY: {source} determines the pathway for conduit, cable tray, or wiring runs through the building during {target}.
verbGlossary.COMPLIANCE-CHECKED BY: {target} verifies {source}'s installation meets code requirements, fire ratings, and life-safety standards.
verbGlossary.SPECIFIED BY: {source} defines the equipment models, cable types, and system architecture for {target}'s low-voltage infrastructure.
verbGlossary.BUILT BY: {target} constructs the framing, partition, or structural element that is the primary deliverable of {source}.
verbGlossary.ASSISTED BY: {target} provides labor support under supervision to accelerate {source}'s installation work.
verbGlossary.LEVELED BY: {source} uses laser or string-line methods to ensure {target}'s ceiling grid, framing, or floor is installed to tolerance.
verbGlossary.PUNCH-LISTED BY: {source} walks the completed phase area and documents deficiencies requiring correction before final acceptance.
verbGlossary.PROTECTED BY: {target} installs temporary coverings or restricts access to preserve {source}'s finished surfaces from damage.
verbGlossary.SET BY: {target} mounts, aligns, and secures the ceiling tiles, trim, or finish components that complete {source}'s installation.
verbGlossary.CATALOGED BY: {source} indexes the hardware sets, finish schedules, or keying matrices that {target} requires for installation.
verbGlossary.SELECTED BY: {source} approves material samples, finish colors, and product selections that define {target}'s aesthetic outcome.
verbGlossary.COMMISSIONED BY: {target} verifies {source}'s mechanical or electrical system operates per design intent through functional performance testing.
verbGlossary.TRIMMED OUT BY: {target} installs the final fixtures, valves, and exposed components that complete {source}'s plumbing or mechanical system.
verbGlossary.ENERGIZED BY: {target} performs the switching, circuit testing, and load verification required to bring {source}'s electrical systems online.
verbGlossary.ACCEPTANCE-TESTED BY: {target} conducts the formal acceptance test that demonstrates {source}'s fire-alarm or life-safety system meets code.
verbGlossary.PROGRAMMED BY: {target} configures the software, firmware, or control sequences that make {source}'s technology systems operational.
verbGlossary.CHECKLISTED BY: {source} develops and executes the pre-functional checklists that confirm {target}'s equipment is ready for startup.
verbGlossary.WITNESSED STARTUP BY: {source} observes the initial system startup to document baseline operating conditions and confirm proper commissioning.
verbGlossary.BALANCED BY: {target} measures and adjusts airflow, water flow, or sound levels to bring {source}'s systems into design specification.
verbGlossary.INTEGRATED BY: {target} tests cross-system interactions (BAS, fire alarm, lighting controls) to verify {source}'s systems work together.
verbGlossary.BENCHMARKED BY: {target} measures {source}'s system performance against design criteria and documents results for owner acceptance.
verbGlossary.MANAGED BY: {target} coordinates the resolution of all outstanding items, tracks completion status, and drives {source} to closure.
verbGlossary.WALKED BY: {source} physically inspects each area during {target} to identify incomplete or non-conforming work items.
verbGlossary.SIGNED OFF BY: {target} provides the final quality approval that confirms {source}'s work meets specification and is ready for occupancy.
verbGlossary.COMPILED BY: {target} assembles the as-built drawings, O&M manuals, and warranty documents into {source}'s closeout deliverable package.
verbGlossary.MODELED AS-BUILT BY: {source} updates the BIM model to reflect field conditions, capturing deviations from design that occurred during {target}.
verbGlossary.ATTENDED BY: {source} participates in {target}'s owner-training sessions to learn building system operations and maintenance procedures.
verbGlossary.RECONCILED BY: {source} resolves outstanding contract balances, change orders, and retention to close out {target}'s financial accounts.
verbGlossary.AUDITED BY: {target} reviews {source}'s financial records, pay applications, and cost documentation for accuracy and completeness.
verbGlossary.ADMINISTERED BY: {source} manages warranty claims, tracks expiration dates, and coordinates callbacks during the post-construction phase.
verbGlossary.TICKETED BY: {source} creates and routes warranty service requests through the project's tracking system during the post-construction phase.
verbGlossary.REBALANCED BY: {source} readjusts airflow or hydronic balancing during seasonal commissioning to optimize year-round system performance.
verbGlossary.OCCUPIED BY: {source} takes beneficial occupancy of the facility, marking the transition from construction to operations.
verbGlossary.HANDED OVER BY: {source} formally transfers project control, keys, and documentation from the construction team to the owner.
verbGlossary.CORRELATES WITH: {target}measure from {source}s domain shows a statistical relationship with phase duration, cost, or quality outcomes across the portfolio.
